blocks.text: Bramblewood Legal PLLC
Patricia Young, General Manager
Foley Group
Re: Engagement for Zoning Variance Petition
Dear Ms. Young:

This letter confirms the engagement of Bramblewood Legal PLLC by {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} to prepare and pursue a petition for a zoning variance affecting the property described in the materials your office has provided. We thank you for entrusting the matter to us. Before any work goes forward under the firm's name, I like a client to see the whole of the engagement on one page: the scope, the people, the fee, and the date we begin.

Our scope is the variance petition and nothing beyond it. We will examine the deed, survey, and recorded instruments for the property; analyze the zoning provisions from which relief is sought and the standards the reviewing board applies; prepare the petition and its supporting exhibits; and appear on Foley Group's behalf at such hearings as the petition requires, through the board's decision. Work outside that scope, an appeal among other possibilities, would proceed only under a written amendment to this letter signed by both of us.

Nicholas Cox, Senior Attorney, will lead the matter and take responsibility for the record. Ashley Reese, Attorney, will prepare the zoning analysis and keep your office informed through regular status reports. Under the firm's standing access rules, the matter folder will be readable by this team and by me, and by no one else at the firm; we treat that restriction with the same seriousness we give a filing deadline.

Our fee is a fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.fee}} for the scope stated above. Filing fees, publication costs, and other third-party charges are additional and will be billed to you as incurred, with documentation. Work will commence on {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}}, and we expect the petition to run its course in approximately four months.

If this letter states the engagement as you understand it, please sign below and return one executed copy to my attention. If it does not, call me before you sign; a scope is easier to fix on paper than in the middle of a hearing. We are glad to have the work.
Memorandum
To: Ashley Reese
From: Nicholas Cox
Re: Kickoff, Zoning Variance Petition for Foley Group

The conclusion first: this petition is won on the record, not at the podium. Our client is {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}}. Our contact is Patricia Young, General Manager, Foley Group. The engagement runs from {{fact:f:E-2019-001.start}} for about four months, through the board's decision. The objective is a petition the board can grant on the papers in front of it, with every parcel fact and every prior filing verified before anyone says a word in a hearing room.

items: Property record. Deed, survey, and every recorded instrument touching the parcel. I own this, and it comes first.
Zoning analysis. The provisions we need relief from and the standards the board applies in granting it. Ashley Reese owns this.
Petition and exhibits. First draft by Ashley Reese; I revise. Nothing is filed until both of us have read it twice.
Client coordination. Working sessions and status reports to Patricia Young's office, in a form her people can forward upward without a call. Ashley Reese owns this.

blocks.text: Next steps. I will circulate the property record summary when the review is finished, not before. Ashley schedules the first working session with the client. The matter folder is restricted to the two of us and the Managing Partner. Keep it that way.
Working Session Minutes: Zoning Variance Petition for Foley Group
Prepared by Ashley Reese, Attorney, Bramblewood Legal PLLC. Circulated to all attendees for correction the same week.

items: Nicholas Cox, Senior Attorney, Bramblewood Legal PLLC
Ashley Reese, Attorney, Bramblewood Legal PLLC (minutes)
Patricia Young, General Manager, Foley Group

blocks.text: Nicholas Cox reported that the property record is closed: the deed, survey, and recorded instruments have been reviewed, and nothing in the record contradicts the facts the draft petition recites. The draft was then read through section by section. Patricia Young confirmed the operational facts stated in the petition and supplied one correction to the description of current use, which was noted in the room and carried into the draft.

Patricia Young asked that future status reports be written so the general counsel of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}} can forward them upward without a covering call, a request the firm is happy to oblige, that being the format we prefer anyway. Discussion closed with agreement on the sequence of filings and no open disputes about the record.

items: Carry the corrected description of current use into the final draft. Owner: Ashley Reese.
Assemble the exhibits in filing order and verify each against the record. Owner: Nicholas Cox.
Prepare the next status report in the format the client requested. Owner: Ashley Reese.
Confirm the board's filing requirements and set the internal schedule. Owner: Nicholas Cox.

blocks.text: Status Report: Zoning Variance Petition for Foley Group
Prepared by Nicholas Cox, Senior Attorney, Bramblewood Legal PLLC, for the executive office of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.client}}.
Status summary

The matter is on schedule and within budget. The engagement remains within the fixed fee of {{fact:f:E-2019-001.fee}} stated in the engagement letter; no adjustment is proposed or anticipated. The petition has been filed with its exhibits complete, the board has accepted it for hearing, and the remaining work is preparation for the hearing itself.
Accomplishments this period

items: Finalized the petition and supporting exhibits; every fact the petition recites was verified against the deed, survey, and recorded instruments before filing.
Filed the petition with the board; the filing was accepted without deficiency.
Ashley Reese delivered the zoning analysis in final form, addressing each standard the board applies to a variance.
Completed the notice and publication steps the filing requires; proof of each has been placed in the matter file.

blocks.text: Upcoming work

items: Prepare the hearing presentation, keyed to the standards addressed in the zoning analysis.
Hold a preparation session with Foley Group so the testimony of its representatives matches the record precisely.
Continue reporting in this format through the board's decision.

blocks.text: Risks

Two risks merit statement. First, the board controls its own calendar; a continuance would extend the engagement's timeline, though not its fee. Second, neighboring owners may appear at the hearing in opposition. The petition was drafted on the assumption that they would, and the record supports each fact it recites. Neither risk calls for action by your office at this time. Questions on this report may be directed to me or to Ashley Reese.
